architectGaps.gap: Empty-state primary CTA for entities admins do not directly create. design-system.md requires the empty state to show “a primary CTA relevant to the entity,” but Orders, Invoices, Shipments, Refunds, Reviews, Support Tickets, and Audit Log are system-generated or customer-originated — there is no conventional “Create X” action to point the CTA at.

Row-action set for immutable / system-of-record entities. The brief's standard row actions are view/edit/delete, but for the Audit Log (immutable, system-generated) edit and delete are semantically invalid.

Navigation targets for the empty-state primary CTA and the view/edit row actions point at detail / create / edit pages that are explicitly out of scope this milestone. No stated convention says whether these affordances should stub, disable, or pre-wire routes to the not-yet-built pages. (Delete is fully in scope and buildable; only the CTA/view/edit targets are affected — this does not hard-block building the tables.)
The standard column set for each of the 10 entities is not defined.
The empty-state primary CTA target for each list page is undefined.
Whether the Audit Log exposes edit/delete row actions or is view-only.

tokens.source: billed = input+output+cache-read+cache-write, summed per-agent from workflow transcripts (agent-*.jsonl), bucketed by config via startedAt order

costUsd.source: cache-aware per-model: Opus 4.8 $5/$25, Sonnet 5 $3/$15 per MTok; cache-write 1.25x input, cache-read 0.1x input (RESULTS.md list rates)
